description: Lock ballistics_and_kinematics_galileo to Galileo's published 1638 projectile-motion and kinematics treatment in Two New Sciences, not the whole Scientific Revolution or the separate strength-of-materials science.

checks.reason: The scoped claim is anchored to the published 1638 Fourth Day treatment of projectile motion in Two New Sciences.
The node remains a mathematical and experimental mechanics result, not a standalone weapon technology.
Galileo's projectile-motion proof uses geometrical decomposition and conic-section reasoning.
Artillery practice supplied the practical projectile problems, but is not a hard component dependency of kinematics.
Construction and strength-of-materials practice belong to a separate Galileo scope from projectile-motion kinematics.
Kepler's laws are neighboring Scientific Revolution work, not a direct prerequisite for Galileo's projectile-motion theorem.
